Myths

Orpheus and Eurydice: Myth, Meaning, and the Rule Against Looking Back

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the underworld journey where love, music, a command, and one backward glance become the whole tragedy.

Myths - Greek Myth - 10 min read - Updated Jul 12, 2026
Myths

Osiris and Isis: Dismemberment, Search, and the Egyptian Myth of Restoration

Osiris and Isis is a pre-existing Myths subject built around the Egyptian myth cycle in which Isis searches for Osiris after his murder and restoration becomes part of kingship and afterlife meaning.

Myths - Egyptian Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 21, 2026
Myths

Pandora's Jar: The Greek Myth Behind Pandora's Box

A remade archive reading of Pandora's jar, the later box tradition, and the question of why hope was left inside.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Persephone and the Underworld: The Greek Myth Behind Loss, Return, and the Seasons

The Persephone myth tells how Persephone is taken to the underworld, how Demeter grieves, and how Persephone returns for part of the year. The cycle is often connected with seasonal change.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Persephone Myth: Underworld, Seasons, and the Meaning of Return

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the Greek myth that links descent, return, grief, agriculture, and the changing year.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 12, 2026
Myths

Phaethon Sun Chariot Myth: Fire, Fall, and the Danger of Driving the Sun

Phaethon is a Greek mythic figure who asks to drive the chariot of the sun and loses control. The burning path threatens the world until Zeus strikes him down.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Prometheus Fire Myth: Theft, Punishment, and the Gift That Changed Humanity

Prometheus is a Greek mythic figure remembered for stealing fire and giving it to humanity. Zeus punishes him for crossing divine limits, often by binding him while an eagle tears at him.

Myths - Greek Myth - 8 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Myths

Prometheus Myth: Fire, Punishment, and the Meaning of a Culture Hero

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the Greek myth of stolen fire, divine punishment, and the dangerous gift that changes human life.

Myths - Greek Myth - 8 min read - Updated Jul 12, 2026

A unified-policy Kyunolab reading of Ra's Solar Boat, based on existing external tradition rather than newly invented archive material.

Ragnarok Norse Myth: Ending, Renewal, and the Meaning of the Final Battle

A source-aware Kyunolab record tracing the Norse mythic ending where gods, monsters, destruction, and renewal are held in the same story.

Myths - Norse Myth - 11 min read - Updated Jul 12, 2026
Myths

Sisyphus Myth: The Stone, the Hill, and the Punishment That Never Ends

Sisyphus is a Greek mythic figure condemned in the underworld to roll a stone uphill, only for it to fall back again.

Myths - Greek Myth - 9 min read - Updated Jul 20, 2026
